ddls:
CREATE TABLE "issues" (
  "issue_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each issue */,
  "summary" TEXT /* Summary of the issue */,
  "item_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the item or service affected */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the category of the issue */,
  "date_time_logged" TEXT /* Date and time the issue was logged */,
  "date_of_last_action" TEXT /* Date and time of the last action taken on the issue */,
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the current status of the issue */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who logged the issue */,
  "priority_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the priority level of the issue */,
  "due_date" TEXT /* Deadline for resolving the issue */,
  "assigned_to_user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user assigned to resolve the issue */,
  "resolution_time" TEXT /* Time taken to resolve the issue */,
  "escalation_level" TEXT /* Level of escalation if the issue is not resolved within the expected time */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity of the issue */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("issue_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_issues_status_id FOREIGN KEY ("status_id") REFERENCES statuses ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_issues_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_issues_assigned_to_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("assigned_to_user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_issues_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES categories ("category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_issues_item_id FOREIGN KEY ("item_id") REFERENCES items ("item_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "items" (
  "item_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each item or service */,
  "item_name" TEXT /* Name of the item or service */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the item or service */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the department associated with the item or service */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance performed on the item or service */,
  "next_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Scheduled date for the next maintenance of the item or service */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("item_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "parent_category_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the parent category */,
  "priority_level" INTEGER /* Default priority level for issues in this category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_categories_parent_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_category_id") REFERENCES categories ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "statuses" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status */,
  "status_name" TEXT /* Name of the status */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the status */,
  "is_final" INTEGER /* Indicates if the status is a final state */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., IT staff, department manager, helpdesk) */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the department associated with the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user for quick contact */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date and time the user last logged into the system */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "actions" (
  "action_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each action */,
  "issue_id" INTEGER /* ID of the issue the action is related to */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who took the action */,
  "action_date" TEXT /* Date and time the action was taken */,
  "action_type" TEXT /* Type of action (e.g., update, resolve) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the action taken */,
  "resolution_time" TEXT /* Time taken to resolve the issue if the action is a resolution */,
  "escalation_level" TEXT /* Level of escalation if the action involves escalation */,
  "impact" TEXT /* Impact of the action */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("action_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_actions_issue_id FOREIGN KEY ("issue_id") REFERENCES issues ("issue_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_actions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
